tldr: Swap CleverTap's mobile engagement suite for Batch, a comparable mobile push and in-app platform with stronger EU/GDPR posture.
why_switch: EU-based vendor with GDPR-native data residency
Freemium tier vs. CleverTap's paid-only entry
Comparable mobile push, segmentation, and A/B testing
export_method: Use CleverTap's REST and Data Export APIs to pull user profiles, events, and subscription state; export templates and campaigns through the CleverTap dashboard. For richer historical event data, request a bulk export through CleverTap support if your plan includes it. Verify token formats and platform credentials are captured.
import_method: Provision a Batch project, configure APNs and FCM credentials, then install Batch's mobile SDKs (iOS/Android) replacing CleverTap's SDK calls. Import user attributes via Batch's profile API and recreate templates and campaigns in the Batch dashboard. Re-author segments using Batch's audience tooling. Always confirm endpoints and SDK shapes against current Batch docs.

concept_mapping.note: Map external_id and attributes; verify naming rules.
Recreate manually in the Batch dashboard.
Audience definitions differ — re-author rather than copy-paste.
Re-import per channel; do not assume defaults.

gotchas: SDK swap requires app store releases — coordinate with mobile release cadence.
Web push support on Batch is secondary to mobile; verify if you rely on it.
Pricing scales aggressively at higher volumes — model your MAU before committing.
Smaller ecosystem than CleverTap — fewer third-party integrations.
Run both SDKs in parallel briefly to validate delivery before sunsetting CleverTap.
Analytics history will not transfer; export benchmarks beforehand.

email.subject: Migrating from CleverTap to Batch
email.body:
Hi team,

Proposing a move from CleverTap to Batch. Batch is an EU-based mobile push and in-app platform with GDPR-native data residency, freemium entry, and comparable feature coverage for our mobile use case.

Plan: export profiles, attributes, push tokens, templates, and segments from CleverTap; install Batch SDKs in iOS/Android; recreate templates and campaigns; run both SDKs in parallel briefly before cutover.

Estimated effort: 3-6 weeks. Main risks: SDK swap requiring app store releases, weaker web push, pricing scaling, and analytics history not transferring.

Let me know if you want to review the cutover checklist.
